behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: This is a module-to-client handoff check (request builder + API adapter). It does not require real network or model judgment; a captured stub request is sufficient. Rejected System because behavior text does not require validating a real external response/error contract or round-trip side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier with a fake API transport that records outbound payload. Invoke classify() with a taxonomy, then assert every valid label is present in the sent request.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at boundary between API response parsing and taxonomy validation. A stubbed API response with an out-of-taxonomy label exercises the real module wiring. Rejected Agentic because correctness here does not depend on model quality/non-determinism, only on defensive mapping logic.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client returning an invalid label (e.g., "medical"). Call classify() and assert returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic normalization logic on fixed input with no I/O. Higher-level checks add no extra reality signal for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization twice with the same raw response fixture and assert identical normalized output each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on live model outputs across repeated calls; stubs cannot reveal real provider variability. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-step agent pipeline with cross-step model-dependent state.
behaviors.test_description: Run N live classifications for identical input, record provider raw responses and final labels, and assert label stability whenever raw provider output is unchanged.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration between tokenizer/budget policy/request builder before outbound dispatch. A fake transport can verify pre-dispatch enforcement and whether dispatch is skipped/modified. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real external contract checks.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget, call classify() with oversized input, and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) plus outbound payload/dispatch behavior via a fake API client.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real provider call and verification of externally returned usage metadata (external happy-path contract). Mock usage fields can hide real tokenizer/provider accounting differences. Rejected Agentic because pass/fail is usage contract/budget compliance, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification against the real provider with representative input, read provider-reported token usage from the real response, and assert usage <= configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is retry orchestration (error handling + backoff + second attempt) across local modules. A controllable fake API that returns rate-limit then success can expose this reliably. Rejected System because behavior text does not explicitly require validating real provider error contract shape or live network timing semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client that returns a rate-limit error on first call and success on second; assert wait/backoff hook was invoked and final result is returned instead of an immediate error.
